item.title: #6: Epic interview with Ex Pro Cyclist Tim Jamieson
item.itunes:title: #6: Epic interview with Ex Pro Cyclist Tim Jamieson
item.description:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

We had the privilege of hearing the epic story of how local Tassie boy Tim Jamieson made it to the pro's in Europe in the 80s, and how the rest of his journey unfolded from there..

Some of his highlights include:
- How he made it onto a pro team as an 18 yr old with no bike and no contacts in Europe
- The inside story of what life on a pro team was really like
- The highs and lows of his career
- Why he got to that level but then why he left the sport for a decade

And more importantly, he gave us some real gold information into the best psychological lessons he learned and how they've applied to the rest of life.

We enjoyed this episode so much it went for over an hour!

Enjoy the listen.

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

item.title: #5: How To Win Even If You're Not The Strongest
item.itunes:title: #5: How To Win Even If You're Not The Strongest
item.description:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

This was a jam packed episode where, in conjunction with one of the best stages of the Tour de France occurring through the epic cobbles of Roubaix, we talk all things racing, and how to use all that work you've been putting into training to get a result in a race.

--> We review one of our favourite stages so far and all the action that happened
--> How to cope mentally with an injury (devastated for Richie Porte)
--> Where to position yourself in a race/bunch
--> Racing to your strengths and weaknesses
--> How to attack
--> How to manage your race and come out on top
--> The ultimate question; How to win even if you're not the strongest rider

Enjoy :)

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.content:encoded:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

This was a jam packed episode where, in conjunction with one of the best stages of the Tour de France occurring through the epic cobbles of Roubaix, we talk all things racing, and how to use all that work you've been putting into training to get a result in a race.

--> We review one of our favourite stages so far and all the action that happened
--> How to cope mentally with an injury (devastated for Richie Porte)
--> Where to position yourself in a race/bunch
--> Racing to your strengths and weaknesses
--> How to attack
--> How to manage your race and come out on top
--> The ultimate question; How to win even if you're not the strongest rider

Enjoy :)

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.
item.itunes:author: TriVelo Coaching

item.title: #4: The Tour Begins and Why Do We Test So Damn Much?
item.itunes:title: #4: The Tour Begins and Why Do We Test So Damn Much?
item.description:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

In this episode we're very excited the greatest race in the world, Le Tour de France has begun! So we discuss our thoughts so far and predictions for the rest of the race. And we talk all things testing, why it's a mandatory part of TriVelo Coaching, why Gerard is so obsessed with it, and how it can really help you achieve your goal.

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.

See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

item.title: #3: Inside the mind of Ex-Olympian Julian Paynter
item.itunes:title: #3: Inside the mind of Ex-Olympian Julian Paynter
item.description:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

We interview Ex-Olympic athlete and now gun masters rider Julian Paynter on what it takes to make it to the olympics, mentally and physically. We cover training, race preparation, handling the pressure in the big races, and what it was really like in the infamous athletes village.

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.

item.title: #2: Is data important? Training with Cadel Evans
item.itunes:title: #2: Is data important? Training with Cadel Evans
item.description:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

In this episode we delve into the new world of technology and data on the bike, exploring what's important and what to ignore. AND Gerard explains how he got so obsessed with data in the first place, thanks to Australia's very own Tour de France winner Cadel Evans.

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.

item.title: #1: Fundamentals of being a better cyclist
item.itunes:title: #1: Fundamentals of being a better cyclist
item.description:
A podcast to help you Train Smarter and Race Faster. Hosted by expert Triathlon coach, Australian Ironman Champion and 15 x Aus Age group Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son, multiple Triathlon + Cycling Aus Age Group Champion Jordy Donnelly.

 

The very first episode of The TriVelo Coaching Podcast! Today we wanted to start big and dive deep into what does it actually take to improve on the bike (or on any training program). No matter what level you're at, there are a mix of factors that will determine whether you can and will become a stronger rider.

The Get Fast Podcast is brought to you by TriVelo Coaching, where we help triathletes and cyclists like you, Train Smarter to Race Faster. You are joined as always by your hosts, Australian Ironman Champion and 4 x Nationals Masters Cycling Champion Gerard Donnelly, and his son Jordan Donnelly.
